hypothesis: Cross-domain citation rate (metric 1: frontier cross-refs) will be near 5.9% (below 6% threshold) at N=900. Denominator growth makes threshold harder over time.

s429_measurement.notes: Same 51 cross-domain lessons as S427 — zero new cross-domain lessons added in 25-lesson interval (S428-S429)

delta.interpretation: DENOMINATOR DRIFT: corpus grew 25 lessons with zero new cross-domain lessons. Threshold target also grew (6% of 900 = 54 vs 6% of 875 = 52.5 = 53 lessons needed). Gap widened from 1-2 lessons to 3 lessons.

structural_finding.pattern: Denominator growth without numerator growth causes progressive metric regression
structural_finding.current_gap: Need 3 more cross-domain lessons to hit 6% (54/900 = 6.0%)
structural_finding.rate_required: New cross-domain lessons at 6%+ of all new lessons to maintain threshold; recent rate = 0/25 = 0%
structural_finding.mechanism: New lessons (L-876..L-900) are domain-specific follow-up work; cross-domain lessons require explicit generalizer sessions targeting ISO atlas or cross-citation

f_exp8_status: NEAR-THRESHOLD (stagnating): 5.67% vs 6% — threshold progressively moving away without active generalizer sessions
f_exp11_rationale: Body-text integration (0.1%) is the unaddressed successor metric. Cites: headers provide awareness (35.9%) but not synthesis. F-EXP11 should target 0.1%→2% body-text integration.
prescription: Open F-EXP11 as successor frontier. Run 1 dedicated generalizer session targeting ISO-1, ISO-4, ISO-6 top-uncited patterns to add 3+ cross-domain lessons and close F-EXP8.
actual: 5.67% (51/900), below 6% threshold; STAGNATING — 0 new cross-domain lessons in last 25
diff: Expected near-threshold. Actual confirms stagnation: same count (51) despite +25 lessons. Denominator growth amplifies gap.
